en.extract:
The yellow-throated Fiji whistler (Pachycephala graeffii) is a species of passerine bird in the family Pachycephalidae, endemic to central Fiji. It was formerly considered to be conspecific with the white-throated Fiji whistler (Pachycephala vitiensis). Before the split the combined species were known as the "Fiji whistler".


Taxonomy
The yellow-throated Fiji whistler was formally described in 1866 by the German ornithologist Gustav Hartlaub based on a specimen collected on the island of Viti Levu in Fiji. He coined the binomial name Pachycephala graeffii where the specific epithet was chosen to honour the Swiss naturalist Eduard Heinrich Graeffe.
The yellow-throated Fiji whistler was formerly considered as conspecific with the white-throated Fiji whistler (Pachycephala vitiensis). It is now treated as a separate species based on the differences in plumage and vocalizations.
Seven subspecies are recognised:

P. g. bella Mayr, 1932 – Vatu Vara (central east Fiji, southwest Polynesia)
P. g. koroana Mayr, 1932 – Koro (central Fiji, southwest Polynesia)
P. g. torquata Layard, EL, 1875 – Taveuni (central north Fiji, southwest Polynesia)
P. g. aurantiiventris Seebohm, 1891 – Yanganga (north of Vanua Levu) and Vanua Levu (except southeast coast; central north Fiji, southwest Polynesia)
P. g. ambigua Mayr, 1932 – southeast Vanua Levu, Rabi and Kioa (east of northeast Vanua Levu; central north Fiji, southwest Polynesia)
P. g. optata Hartlaub, 1866 – southeast coast Viti Levu and Ovalau (west Lomaiviti group; west, central west Fiji, southwest Polynesia)
P. g. graeffii Hartlaub, 1866 – Waya (Yasawa Islands group) and Viti Levu (northwest, west Fiji, southwest Polynesia)

The '''yellow-throated Fiji whistler''' ('''''Pachycephala graeffii''''') is a species of [[passerine]] bird in the family [[Pachycephalidae]], [[Endemism|endemic]] to central [[Fiji]]. It was formerly considered to be [[conspecific]] with the [[white-throated Fiji whistler]] (''Pachycephala vitiensis''). Before the split the combined species were known as the "Fiji whistler".

==Taxonomy==
The yellow-throated Fiji whistler was [[species description|formally described]] in 1866 by the German ornithologist [[Gustav Hartlaub]] based on a specimen collected on the island of [[Viti Levu]] in [[Fiji]]. He coined the [[binomial name]] ''Pachycephala graeffii'' where the specific epithet was chosen to honour the Swiss naturalist [[Eduard Heinrich Graeffe]].<ref>{{ cite journal | last=Hartlaub | first=Gustav | author-link=Gustav Hartlaub | date=1866 | title=On five new species of birds from the Feejee Islands | journal=Ibis | series=2nd series | volume=2 | pages=171–173 [172] | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/56940943 }}</ref><ref>{{ cite book | editor-last=Paynter | editor-first=Raymond A. Jr | year=1986 | title=Check-List of Birds of the World | volume=12 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology | location=Cambridge, Massachusetts | page=30 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14482147 }}</ref><ref>{{ cite web | last=Jobling | first=James A. | title=graeffii | work=The Key to Scientific Names | url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/key-to-scientific-names/search?q=graeffii | publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology | access-date=7 September 2024 }}</ref>

The yellow-throated Fiji whistler was formerly considered as [[conspecific]] with the [[white-throated Fiji whistler]] (''Pachycephala vitiensis''). It is now treated as a separate species based on the differences in plumage and vocalizations.<ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela C. Rasmussen | date=August 2024 | title=Whiteheads, sittellas, Ploughbill, Australo-Papuan bellbirds, Shriketit, whistlers | work=IOC World Bird List Version 14.2 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/whistlers/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=20 September 2024 }}</ref>

Seven subspecies are recognised:<ref name=ioc/>
* ''P. g. bella'' [[Ernst Mayr|Mayr]], 1932 – [[Vatu Vara]] (central east [[Fiji]], southwest [[Polynesia]])
* ''P. g. koroana'' Mayr, 1932 – [[Koro Island|Koro]] (central [[Fiji]], southwest [[Polynesia]])
* ''P. g. torquata'' [[Edgar Leopold Layard|Layard, EL]], 1875 – [[Taveuni]] (central north [[Fiji]], southwest [[Polynesia]])
* ''P. g. aurantiiventris'' [[Henry Seebohm|Seebohm]], 1891 – Yanganga (north of [[Vanua Levu]]) and [[Vanua Levu]] (except southeast coast; central north [[Fiji]], southwest [[Polynesia]])
* ''P. g. ambigua'' Mayr, 1932 – southeast [[Vanua Levu]], [[Rabi Island|Rabi]] and [[Kioa]] (east of northeast [[Vanua Levu]]; central north [[Fiji]], southwest [[Polynesia]])
* ''P. g. optata'' [[Gustav Hartlaub|Hartlaub]], 1866 – southeast coast [[Viti Levu]] and [[Ovalau]] (west [[Lomaiviti Islands|Lomaiviti group]]; west, central west [[Fiji]], southwest [[Polynesia]])
* ''P. g. graeffii'' Hartlaub, 1866 – [[Waya Island|Waya]] ([[Yasawa Islands]] group) and [[Viti Levu]] (northwest, west [[Fiji]], southwest [[Polynesia]])
